warnings: Electronic ΔG_solv only — no cavitation, dispersion-repulsion, or thermal correction. This is an electronic-energy difference E(solv) − E(gas), not a thermodynamic free energy (no ZPE/entropy).

Standard-state caveat: the value is NOT corrected to the conventional ΔG*_solv 1 M (gas) → 1 M (solution) state; the experimental tables you may compare against include a ≈1.9 kcal mol⁻¹ (RT ln 24.46) term this number omits. Do not compare directly to tabulated ΔG*_solv without adding it.
Semi-empirical implicit solvation is screening-grade; ±2–3 kcal mol⁻¹ typical.

Single-conformer result: every species was evaluated at ONE geometry, with no conformational/Boltzmann averaging. For a flexible molecule the Boltzmann-averaged free energy over conformers can differ from a single conformer by several kcal mol⁻¹ — shifting pK_a by >1 unit, E° by >0.1 V, and logP by >0.5.

prose:
Phenol electronic solvation free energy  
Property: ΔG_solv (electronic) = –3.6506569 kcal mol⁻¹

Method provenance:  
• Level of theory: GFN2-xTB semi-empirical tight-binding  
• Geometry: single-point on the supplied phenol.xyz (no separate gas/solvent optimizations)  
• Solvent model: ALPB implicit water (continuum); gas-phase reference explicitly stated  
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (neutral singlet)  
• Backend: xtb 6.x via chemkit solvation skill  
• Convergence: SCF converged; result flagged trustworthy by chemkit integrity check  
• Warnings surfaced verbatim above (electronic-only ΔG_solv, standard-state and conformer caveats)

Live log was written during execution to a timestamped .out file as per chemkit standards, along with the JSON result for full reproducibility.
